roles.name: Show About Nothing Architect
roles.description: Designs the over-arching premise — what's the deal with this Salesforce solution?

roles.name: Master of His Domain
roles.description: Holds the timeline, the calendar, and the regifted commitments — runs the show like a Soup Nazi.

roles.name: Vault Manager
roles.description: Keeper of the vault — secrets, fields, permission sets, and who's been deleted from the contact list.

roles.name: Festivus Engineer
roles.description: Builds Apex and LWC for the rest of us — airs grievances about technical debt.

roles.name: Latex Salesman Lead
roles.description: Senior partner over all stateside Salesforce craft — like Vandelay Industries, but real.

roles.name: Bizarro Senior Consultant
roles.description: Mirror-image consultant from across the world — does everything Jerry would, just opposite.

roles.name: Bizarro Consultant
roles.description: Junior bizarro — picks up discovery and configuration with very different methods.

roles.name: Subway Conductor Architect
roles.description: Architect of the underground tunnels between Salesforce and the rest of NYC's systems.

roles.name: Mailman Senior Integrator
roles.description: Senior letter-carrier — delivers payloads through middleware come rain, sleet, or Newman.

roles.name: Bike Messenger Integrator
roles.description: Apprentice carrier — webhooks, batches, and getting the package across town before the meter runs out.

roles.name: Pretzel Tester
roles.description: These pretzels are making me thirsty — finds every defect that makes a release go stale.

roles.name: J. Peterman Catalog Lead
roles.description: Lifts the banner of the brand — narrates every campaign with the gravitas of a man who's been to Burma.

roles.name: Catalog Senior Copywriter
roles.description: Seasoned wordsmith for the catalog — turns a simple shirt into a tale of windswept adventure.

roles.name: Catalog Copywriter
roles.description: Junior copy — drafts campaigns and the daily catalog content under Peterman's eye.

roles.name: Importer/Exporter
roles.description: Importer AND exporter — translates between the customer's world and the engineering crew. Like Art Vandelay.

roles.name: Master Cosmetologist
roles.description: Senior craftsman of the build — has the level (no longer an assistant).

roles.name: Kramerica Intern
roles.description: Bright-eyed intern at Kramerica Industries — learns the trade on the oil-tanker bladder system project.

_realmNotes: windowOffsetWeeks values are negative so every realm starts at least one month in the past — past weeks get Time Entries (Step 6), future weeks get Schedules. Workstreams are organised as parent scope items (each with 2-3 child sub-scopes) — the wizard inserts parents first, then children with Parent_WorkStream__c set.

children.name: No Soup For You Load Test

core-four.name: Pilot Pitch to NBC
core-four.description: Discovery sign-off — the show about nothing gets greenlit.

core-four.name: Move into Apartment 5A
core-four.description: Build kickoff: the set is dressed and the cereal cabinet is stocked.

core-four.name: The Contest
core-four.description: Mid-project gate — discipline review: who is still master of their domain?

core-four.name: The Marine Biologist Invoice
core-four.description: Phase invoice trigger — the sea was angry that day, my friends.

core-four.name: Series Finale Go-Live
core-four.description: Go-Live: the foursome stand trial in Latham, MA. System cuts over.

core-four.name: Hyper-Care in Holding Cell
core-four.description: Hyper-care close: support during the post-go-live sentence.

costanza-family.name: Festivus Pole Raised
costanza-family.description: Project kickoff — an aluminum pole, no tinsel, the season begins.

costanza-family.name: Vandelay Industries Incorporated
costanza-family.description: Build phase: latex sales pipeline live, architect persona ready.

costanza-family.name: Airing of Grievances Review
costanza-family.description: UAT gate — Frank lists how the project has disappointed him this year.

costanza-family.name: Feats of Strength Cutover
costanza-family.description: Go-Live: Festivus is not over until the head of household is pinned.

costanza-family.name: Serenity Now Hyper-Care
costanza-family.description: Hyper-care close: insanity later, but for now, serenity now.

costanza-family.name: Final Invoice from Costanza & Son
costanza-family.description: Final invoice — Frank insists on cash, Estelle insists on a check.

peterman-pendant.name: Catalog Concept Approved
peterman-pendant.description: Discovery: the Peterman voice and pendant SKU sign-off.

peterman-pendant.name: Burma Sourcing Trip
peterman-pendant.description: Build: Peterman returns from Myanmar with the pendant prototype.

peterman-pendant.name: Editor's Acceptance Review
peterman-pendant.description: Solution review with J. Peterman — the prose must sing.

peterman-pendant.name: Catalog Mails Out
peterman-pendant.description: Go-Live: the spring catalog hits every mailbox in the tri-state area.

peterman-pendant.name: Returns Window Hyper-Care
peterman-pendant.description: Project close: the returns desk ramps down.

peterman-pendant.name: Final Catalog Invoice
peterman-pendant.description: Final invoice — paid out of the petty cash drawer Elaine controls.

nyc-recurring.name: Sidewalk Survey Sign-Off
nyc-recurring.description: Kickoff at Tom's Restaurant — the recurring cast assembles.

nyc-recurring.name: Kramerica Bladder Prototype
nyc-recurring.description: Discovery: the oil tanker bladder system enters first build.

nyc-recurring.name: Soup Stand Opens for UAT
nyc-recurring.description: Build: the Soup Nazi opens his line for end-user testing — order correctly or no soup.

nyc-recurring.name: Jackie Chiles Legal Review
nyc-recurring.description: UAT acceptance — outrageous, egregious, preposterous… and signed off.

nyc-recurring.name: Citywide Network Go-Live
nyc-recurring.description: Go-Live + handoff: the recurring cast network goes live across Manhattan.

nyc-recurring.name: Final Settlement
nyc-recurring.description: Final invoice — Chiles takes a third, the rest goes to the co-op.

Jerry Seinfeld.notes: Stand-up gig at the Improv — pre-booked road dates.

George Costanza.notes: Napping under the desk at Yankee Stadium — do not disturb.

Elaine Benes.notes: Sponge inventory crisis — must shop the East Side dry.

Cosmo Kramer.notes: Kramerica off-site — testing the bladder system in the harbor.

Newman.notes: Postal route reroute — when you control the mail, you control information.

Frank Costanza.notes: Cooking kasha for the Festivus dinner — do not interrupt!

Estelle Costanza.notes: Doctor's appointment — won't say what for.

Susan Ross.notes: Wedding invitation envelope licking — strictly off-grid.

J. Peterman.notes: Sabbatical in Burma — Elaine running the catalog in his absence.

Mr. Pitt.notes: Eating a Snickers bar with a knife and fork — uninterrupted.

The Soup Nazi.notes: Soup recipe retreat — top secret. NO SOUP TODAY!

David Puddy.notes: Mechanic's certification renewal — and a Devils game.
